hypothesis: Contrastive multimodal pretraining between SMILES and 3D electron density grids will induce emergent stereochemical discriminability—distinguishing enantiomers and diastereomers that share identical SMILES canonical representations but differ in 3D spatial arrangement—when the symbolic anchor is replaced with or augmented by stereochemistry-aware descriptors (e.g., InChI strings encoding R/S configurations), such that the resulting latent space achieves lower Davies–Bouldin indices for chiral cluster separation than SE(3)-equivariant architectures trained without symbolic supervision, thereby resolving the paper's acknowledged limitation that rigid SO(3) invariance assumptions break down for stereochemically sensitive molecules.
